title: Green Taxes in a Post-Paris World: Are Millions of Nays Inevitable?
authors: Stefano Carattini; Andrea Baranzini; Philippe Thalmann; Frédéric Varone; Frank Vöhringer

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: energy tax (replacing VAT) / tax on non-renewable energy
tax_mentions.context: Swiss 2015 initiative replaced VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy; determinants of voters’ yes/no choice.

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: carbon tax (CHF/tCO2) with revenue recycling options
tax_mentions.context: Choice experiment presents carbon tax with 4 tax rates and revenue recycling (income tax rebates, VAT rebates, lump-sum, social cushioning, environmental earmarking via offsets).

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: current Swiss carbon tax on heating and process fuels (status quo)
tax_mentions.context: Status quo carbon tax on heating and process fuels (84 CHF/tCO2) with current redistribution; included as an alternative in the choice experiment.

inclusion_reason: Uses Swiss real ballot data and a full-information choice experiment to study public support/vote choice for an energy tax replacing VAT and for carbon taxes (CHF/tCO2) with different revenue-recycling designs.
